identity.title: Merge peer reviews into the executable architecture design

authorization.reference: Operator requested a new project-start design file that merges all peer reviews into the original design, improves the yt-dlp-derived Rust architecture, enforces architecture and coding habits through build rules, prevents the independent diagnostic watcher from going stale, and incorporates research-backed Ponytail critiques and YAGNI principles.

lifecycle.next_action: Closed. A new work packet may implement the Phase 0 executable-gate bootstrap defined by v0.3.0.

scope.summary: Create the next canonical Ferric Forager design by preservation-first integration of the v0.2.0 design, all peer reviews, current architecture research, Ponytail/YAGNI heuristics, executable architecture rules, and watcher anti-staleness requirements.
in_scope: Preservation-first v0.2.0 to v0.3.0 design revision with review traceability
Coarse Rust workspace architecture and dependency-direction contracts
Executable architecture and coding-quality enforcement requirements
Independent watcher lifecycle, freshness, compatibility, and regression contracts
Current Ponytail critique and YAGNI research distilled into project heuristics
Authority routing, topology, registry, and build-rule synchronization required by the new design
non_goals: Reducing the final extractor, protocol, metadata, archive, diagnostics, or expanded-product scope
Authorizing new production runtimes or native dependencies
Claiming full implementation readiness or yt-dlp replacement parity
dependencies: Current primary-source software architecture and Rust tooling research

acceptance_matrix.requirement: The v0.3.0 design preserves the complete v0.2.0 product scope and explicitly traces every primary-synthesis and lens-review finding to an accepted change, retained decision, deferred proof gate, or documented rejection.
evidence: All 39 original H1 sections and core scope markers are retained; Appendix E contains the exact 47 SYN/AP/FCP/SR rows; independent design re-audit resolved 14/15 substantive findings with only Git tracking pending closure.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: The architecture defines coarse Rust components, allowed dependency directions, public boundary contracts, state-machine ownership, and split criteria that can be checked mechanically without freezing premature crate granularity.
evidence: Sections 8.5, 8.6, and 9 define contracts/engine/adapters/composition rings, facade, launcher, Handshake adapter, state ownership, split triggers, separate exception authority, and graph/source/runtime/artifact proof classes.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Canonical build rules require executable architecture conformance, disciplined Rust coding defaults, exception handling, and CI evidence rather than prose-only compliance.
evidence: build-rules.yaml contains contiguous FF-BUILD-001..076, five exact repository-root Cargo gates, change-evidence schema, fail-closed exception allowlist, tooling-policy preflight, and truthful REQUIRED_NOT_YET_IMPLEMENTED status.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Watcher requirements prevent diagnostic staleness through versioned protocol compatibility, feature/incident coverage, freshness signals, end-to-end fixtures, lifecycle tests, and release gates while preserving process and correctness independence.
evidence: Sections 25.6-25.8 and 26.10 plus FF-BUILD-068..076 define degraded lifecycle independence, typed loop progress, privacy, reserved critical storage and residual gaps, independent inventory/corpora/replay, N/N-1 platform coverage, and adversarial tests; final watcher audit PASS.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: The design records current primary-source Ponytail/YAGNI research, critiques, selected heuristics, rejected interpretations, risks, mitigations, and validation implications.
evidence: Sections 6.13-6.16 and references R18-R23/R28-R29 pin Ponytail commit 16f2980, separate source claims from Ferric adaptations, include corrected and independent critiques, and enforce scope-before-simplicity with three-stage rerun evidence; final YAGNI audit PASS.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: START_HERE.yaml, project Codex authority, topology, ID registry, taskboard, build rules, and the new design agree on authority paths, stable IDs, and active work state.
evidence: START_HERE.yaml, .GOV/codex.yaml, .GOV/topology.yaml, .GOV/id-registry.yaml, taskboard, packet, build rules, and v0.3.0 resolve the same design and active packet IDs; AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md remain byte-identical.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: All changed YAML and JSON parse, Markdown topic structure and review traceability validate, rule and registry IDs remain consistent, and adversarial negative-path checks expose unresolved uncertainty without false readiness claims.
evidence: Six YAML and three JSON files parsed; 76 build rules and five gate IDs aligned; prose topic/frontmatter passed; 39 H1 sections and 47 findings passed; authority/state, portability, allowed-path, adversarial wording, and git diff checks passed. Canonical Cargo gate correctly reports product/Cargo.toml absent and remains unimplemented.

checks_run: six YAML and three JSON files parse
FF-BUILD-001..076 and five registered gate IDs align
39 original H1 sections and all 47 review rows retained
authority, active-state, topic, portability, allowed-path, and adversarial contract probes pass
independent design audit: all 15 findings resolved after closure commit 1144bb93ba7469f844bf8a950e8e102455f3373d tracked v0.3.0 atomically
canonical architecture command: expected failure because product/Cargo.toml does not yet exist
last_checkpoint.next_action: Closed. A new work packet may implement the Phase 0 executable-gate bootstrap defined by v0.3.0.

not_proven: Execution of FF-GATE-ARCH-001, FF-GATE-PR-001, FF-GATE-DEEP-001, FF-GATE-RELEASE-001, or FF-GATE-WATCHER-001 before the Phase 0 product bootstrap exists

required_outcomes: preserve the complete v0.2.0 product scope
integrate every peer-review finding with explicit disposition
enforce coding discipline and change evidence through build rules
keep the independent watcher current without making it a correctness dependency
distill current Ponytail critiques and YAGNI into guarded heuristics
risk_cases: bounded diagnostic storage making absolute losslessness impossible
review dispositions exceeding their normative controls

reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.result: rejected because the accepted request requires one merged starting surface
reused byte-for-byte as the preservation base before in-place v0.3.0 integration

reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.candidate: reuse existing project authority and review corpus
reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.result: selected for routing, IDs, state, and all 47 finding anchors

reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.candidate: reuse standard Cargo metadata and Rust workspace lint surfaces
reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.result: selected for graph and lint evidence within their documented limits

reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.candidate: reuse approved dependencies or install Ponytail
reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.result: rejected; no product dependency is needed and Ponytail is research input, not authority
rejected because the complete traceable design and atomic rules cannot remain clear or complete in that form
selected as one v0.3.0 design plus synchronized canonical build rules and authority routing

artifact_specific_justification.technical_design: A new versioned source is required to preserve v0.2.0 while making review integration and supersession explicit.
artifact_specific_justification.build_rules: Canonical atomic rules are required because prose architecture and watcher promises alone cannot enforce later product changes.
artifact_specific_justification.authority_routing: START_HERE, Codex authority, topology, and registry must resolve the same canonical design atomically.
artifact_specific_justification.work_packet_evidence: The active packet is the existing canonical per-change machine-readable surface; a second evidence file would create drift.

change_evidence.pre_simplification_verdict: FAIL_THEN_CORRECTED: independent audits found bootstrap circularity, scope/identity/security/sink gaps, YAGNI escape hatches, unbootstrappable commands, watcher independence contradictions, and proof overclaims.
simplification_changes: replaced premature fine-grained architecture with coarse rings and evidence-triggered splits
made one active packet the canonical change-evidence record
derived machine facts from Cargo and retained only human decisions in architecture policy
replaced timestamps and self-agreement with independent watcher inventory, canonical corpus, and current-binary replay
replaced absolute losslessness with scoped durable admission guarantees and explicit residual gaps
change_evidence.post_simplification_verdict: PASS: the complete accepted scope, risk, review-traceability, authority, architecture, coding, YAGNI, and watcher matrix was rerun after all simplification and adversarial corrections.
complete_post_change_verification: 39 original top-level sections and core scope markers
independent design, watcher/build, and Ponytail/YAGNI re-audits

ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.current_status_limit: Design and build-rule contracts are authored; product workspace, xtask, architecture policy, tooling policy, watcher, and all executable product gates do not yet exist.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.next_required_closure: The first product implementation work packet must create the Phase 0 bootstrap closure before any shipped crate.
